samples.gvd:
module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);

reg [WIDTH-1:0] ram [0:DEPTH-1];

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
    end
    else begin
        if (read_en) begin
            read_data <= ram[read_addr];
        end
        else begin
            read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}};
        end
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        if (write_en) begin
            ram[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
    else begin
        if (write_en) begin
            ram[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description: Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. 6 8 Loop variable for the for loop. Initialize the RAM array to all zeros. for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin RAM[i] = {WIDTH{1'b0}}; end Reset the read_data register to 0. if (!rst_n) begin read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}}; end Write data to the RAM array at the specified write address when write_en is 1. if (write_en) begin RAM[write_addr] <= write_data; end Read data from the RAM array at the specified read address when read_en is 1. if (read_en) begin read_data <= RAM[read_addr]; end Reset the read_data register to 0. else begin read_data <= {WIDTH{1'b0}}; end
